tags.description: Drift dive site with strong currents. Also known as Batu Tiga, requires Advanced Open Water certification. Sloping reefs, craggy rocks and walls covered in hard and soft corals. Visibility often reaches 30 meters. Strong and unpredictable currents especially during full moon. White-tip reef sharks, wobbegong sharks, turtles, mola mola (Jul-Oct).
tags.name: Gili Mimpang

tags.description: US Army cargo ship launched 1918, torpedoed by Japanese submarine I-66 on 11 January 1942 while carrying railway parts and rubber. Beached at Tulamben same day; slid into sea during 1963 Mount Agung eruption. Lies parallel to shore ~25m offshore, spanning 3-30m depth. Wikipedia records length as ~125m (411ft 6in); dive operators commonly cite 120m. Wreck hull encrusted in soft corals, gorgonian fans, sponges. Marine life: bigeye trevally, bumphead parrotfish (night/dawn), Napoleon wrasse, turtles, reef sharks, great barracuda, garden eels.
tags.name: USAT Liberty Wreck

tags.description: North side of Menjangan Island in West Bali National Park. Shallow reef slopes from 5m to 40m+. Rich hard and soft corals, table corals, gorgonian fans, barrel sponges. Clownfish in anemones throughout shallows. Bigeye trevally, titan triggerfish, scorpionfish, sea turtles, garden eels, large grouper. Sharks (whitetip) occasionally below 20m.
tags.name: Coral Garden

tags.description: South/east wall of Menjangan Island. Cave entrances at 9-12m depth, wall drops to 60m. Bats inhabit caves at waterline giving site its name. Cave crevasses harbour nudibranchs, electric clams, moray eels, crustaceans. Wall section: large gorgonians, whip corals, barrel sponges. Schools of bigeye trevally, blacktip reef sharks at depth, octopus. Pygmy seahorses on gorgonians 20-30m. Typically dived as continuation from Pos 2 drift.
tags.name: Bat Cave

tags.description: Southwest coast of Nusa Penida. Manta ray cleaning stations at 8-15m; site extends to 35m on wall. Near year-round manta encounters (reef manta rays). Almost 10% of Nusa Penida manta population are melanistic (all-black) - documented at this site. Confirmed species: bamboo sharks, blue-spotted stingrays, blacktip reef sharks, turtles, nudibranchs, banded sea krait, crown jellyfish, mola mola (seasonal), whale sharks (rare, documented). Boat only; fully exposed to Indian Ocean swell.
tags.name: Manta Point

tags.description: Jemeluk Bay, Amed. Balinese temple sculpture on sandy seabed marked by surface buoy. Batfish gather beneath shrine structure. Jemeluk Bay reef hosts lionfish, clownfish in anemones, butterflyfish, triggerfish, stingrays, schools of big-mouth mackerel, garden eels. Coral-covered reef around bay perimeter 10-25m.
tags.name: Underwater Shrine

tags.description: Reef dive site near Nusa Penida/south Bali area. NOTE: Name 'Bali Scuba' does not appear as a named dive site in operator listings; may be an OSM entry for a dive business rather than a named dive site. No site-specific sources found.
tags.name: Bali Scuba

tags.description: Likely the house reef at Bluehill Dive Resort Tulamben, a shallow shore-entry reef (2-15m) between the USAT Liberty Wreck and the Tulamben Wall. Known for statues and stone carvings scattered on the reef. Single source only; requires additional independent validation.
tags.name: Blue Hill Dive Site

tags.description: Reef dive site near Nusa Penida area. NOTE: 'Water Walk' at Sanur is primarily known as a seawalker (helmet diving) activity venue, not a conventional scuba dive site. No scuba dive site named 'Water Walk' confirmed in operator listings at these coordinates. Requires verification.
tags.name: Water Walk

tags.description: Western side of Menjangan Island. Sandy-bottomed cavern at 14m lit by ambient light. Cave walls: sponges, electric clams (flash in dim light), small invertebrates. Outside: large grouper, frogfish, anglerfish in overhangs. Pygmy seahorses on gorgonian fans near cave entrance. Wall with gullies and channels. Generally mild currents on this side.
tags.name: Underwater Cave

tags.description: Corner point on Menjangan Island where reef wall turns sharply. Currents accelerate around corner. Wall drops vertically from surface to 30m+, covered in gorgonian fans, black coral trees, soft corals. Dense fish aggregations: bigeye trevally, snapper, fusiliers. Blacktip reef sharks at 20-30m. Turtles, large grouper, moray eels. Pygmy seahorses on gorgonians.
tags.name: Blue Corner

tags.description: Menjangan Island main entrance, in front of West Bali National Park ranger station. Gentle sandy slope to 30m. Jetty pilings: ghost pipefish, pipefish, nudibranchs, crustaceans. Sandy slope: garden eels, blue-spotted stingrays, cuttlefish. Batfish near structure. Calm conditions, excellent for training dives.
tags.name: Post I Jetty

tags.description: Western tip of Menjangan Island. Wall drops to ~30m. Good morning light through shallow corals. Dense juvenile fish nurseries in shallow branching corals. Lionfish, ghost pipefish, moray eels on wall. Turtles feeding on reef crest. Reef sharks and trevally at depth. Batfish mid-water. Slightly more sheltered than eastern wall sites.
tags.name: Mangrove Point

tags.description: Northwest Nusa Penida near Broken Beach (Pasih Uug). Sandy bottom slopes from 5m to 12m then to 30m. Reef mantas often seen at 1-6m, no fixed cleaning stations. ~80% encounter rate. Blue-spotted stingrays, reef sharks, turtles on coral bommies. Mola mola possible July-October. Less exposed than Manta Point. Clownfish, moray eels on bommies.
tags.name: Manta Bay

tags.description: Shore-entry wall dive along Amed's northeast Bali coastline. Reef slope drops to wall at ~5m, descends to ~25m. Hard and soft corals, sea fans, sponges. Fusiliers, angelfish, surgeonfish, hawksbill turtles, reef whitetip sharks at depth. Macro: ghost pipefish, frogfish, nudibranchs in sea fans and muck base. Visibility 10-25m. Usually mild currents.
tags.name: Amed wall

tags.description: Sheltered bay east of Padangbai ferry port, east Bali. White sandy bottom with coral bommies starting at 5m, wall section (Tanjung Sari) to 15-18m. Shore entry from beach. Ideal for Open Water certification. Clownfish in anemones abundant. Moray eels, lionfish, sweetlips, turtles, reef sharks on wall, nudibranchs. Night dives: octopus, cuttlefish, lobster. Can have unexpected currents.
tags.name: Blue Lagoon

tags.description: Northern section of Gili Tepekong in Amuk Bay. Less challenging than the south canyon. Huge boulders to swim among and open coral plains. Moderate currents possible but manageable for intermediate divers. Boat dive from Padangbai. Reef and boulder formations 10-25m.
tags.name: Gili Tepekong North

tags.description: The Canyon ('the toilet') - southern section of Gili Tepekong. Dramatic drop-off with swim-through tunnel (28m to 18m), shark boulder, canyon formation. Strong to very strong currents with dangerous downcurrents. Bali's most technically demanding dive site. Whitetip/blacktip reef sharks, sweetlips schools, jacks, groupers, tuna, mola mola (Jul-Oct). Advanced OW + 50 logged dives minimum.
tags.name: Gili Tepekong South

tags.description: Northwest tip of Nusa Penida. Bali's premier site for mola mola (ocean sunfish) July-October; peak August-September. Two shallow reefs on either side of bay plus deep channel to 190m. Powerful downcurrents documented causing multiple accidents and fatalities - advanced divers only. Water temperature drops to 16-18°C during cold upwellings. Mola mola cleaning stations at 20-35m. Also: manta rays, barracuda, giant trevally, whitetip/blacktip reef sharks, turtles, frogfish, wobbegong, nudibranchs.
tags.name: Crystal Bay

tags.description: North coast of Nusa Penida, near sacred Ped temple. Drift dive along wall with wide shallow plateau. Turtles regularly on plateau. Whitetip reef sharks, barracuda, mola mola (Jul-Oct peak). Moderate drift current; pull-out currents and occasional downdrafts possible. Visibility 25-30m. Boat from Toyapakeh (15-20 min). Links seamlessly with SD Point.
tags.name: Ped

tags.description: Shore-entry reef in Amed area. Coral clump rising from sandy bottom. Nutrient-rich Lombok Strait waters. Schools of snappers, platax (batfish), fusiliers. Tuna in blue water. Giant frogfish and leaf scorpionfish confirmed. Nudibranchs, crabs, mantis shrimps. 5-30m depth. Nil to moderate current. Good visibility 15-30m.
tags.name: Batu Belah

tags.description: Ghost Bay - muck dive in Amed area. Black volcanic sand bay with burrows, rubble, sparse coral. Garden eel colonies. Flying gurnard (iconic species). Blue-spotted stingrays, frogfish, mantis shrimp, nudibranchs, cuttlefish, ghost pipefish. 8-25m depth. Mild current. Note: only 2 sources found; further validation recommended.
tags.name: Bulakan

tags.description: Jemeluk Bay, Amed. Hollow concrete pyramid structures placed by local fishermen in early 1990s as reef enhancement. Now covered in soft corals, sea fans, hard coral. ~18-22m depth. Hawksbill and green turtles at cleaning stations. Blue-spotted stingrays on sandy floor. Frogfish, leaf/stone scorpionfish on pyramids. Pygmy seahorses on pink sea fans. Shore or boat access.
tags.name: Pyramids

tags.description: Shore-entry shallow reef between Liberty Wreck and Drop-off at Tulamben. 3-15m depth. Table corals, staghorn, massive Porites heads on black volcanic sand. Sunken Balinese statues (Buddha, Hindu deities) as focal points. Ghost pipefish, leaf scorpionfish, harlequin shrimp, mantis shrimp, clownfish in large anemone patches, ribbon eels, reef sharks, bumphead parrotfish (early morning). Night: Spanish dancers. Accessible all levels.
tags.name: Coral Garden Tulamben

tags.description: Tulamben area. Note: 'Big Fins TecDiving' refers to a technical diving operator (bigfins.asia) rather than a named dive site. No independent dive site named 'Big Fins TecDiving' confirmed in operator site listings. Single source; requires verification as a legitimate dive site entry.
tags.name: Big Fins TecDiving

tags.description: Southeast point of Menjangan Island. Wall from 12m to 40m+. Most spectacular coral 15-25m: largest sea fans in Bali, whip corals, multi-coloured sponges. Denise pygmy seahorses on gorgonians 20-25m - one of best pygmy seahorse spots in Bali. Spotted eagle rays in mid-water. Dense fusiliers, triggerfish, trevally. Turtles. Cold upwellings; occasional mola mola and manta rays. Connects to Bat Cave for long drift.
tags.name: Menjangan - Pos 2

tags.description: Southeast of Tulamben Bay, ~5 min by jukung. Sloping reef 12-30m+. Hard corals, large barrel sponges, gorgonian sea fans with pygmy seahorses (up to 10 per fan). Garden eels, sweetlips, angelfish, bannerfish, turtles, groupers, cuttlefish, trevally, bumphead parrotfish. Reef sharks at 20-30m. Submerged chair and table as artificial reef on sandy slope. Mild-moderate drift possible.
tags.name: Emerald
Gili Biaha
tags.description: Most remote of Amuk Bay's three Gili islands; ~45 min boat from Padangbai. Black volcanic wall surrounding island drops to 40m+. Shark Cave at ~10-12m depth on northeastern face where whitetip reef sharks rest; schools of glass fish at cave entrance. Strong and unpredictable currents including downcurrents. Confirmed species: whitetip reef sharks, lobsters, crabs, pufferfish, nudibranchs, octopus, cuttlefish, frogfish, leaf scorpionfish, lionfish, turtles, mola mola (seasonal), dolphins. Best Jun-Oct.

tags.name: Drop-off
tags.description: Tulamben Wall - volcanic wall formed by Mount Agung lava flow. Shore entry across pebble beach. Wall top 3-5m drops to 60m+. Shallow aquarium at 5m teems with reef fish. Wall: sponges, gorgonian fans, black coral, crinoids. Whitetip reef sharks, Napoleon wrasse, frogfish, ghost pipefish, nudibranchs. Adjacent to USAT Liberty wreck.

tags.name: Harlequin Slope
tags.description: Black sand muck slope ~200m southeast of USAT Liberty wreck. Named for resident harlequin shrimps found under rocks at depth. Volcanic sand ridges with small coral outcrops. Shore entry. Single source only - requires additional independent validation for 3-source requirement.

tags.name: Seraya Secrets
tags.description: ~3km SE of Tulamben near Seraya village. Three sub-sites: Top Secrets (shallow), Deep Secrets (to ~35m), Noisy Reef (juvenile triggerfish sounds). Silty black volcanic sand - strict buoyancy required. Metal dome artificial structure colonised by soft corals. Confirmed species: nudibranchs (donut, pikachu), harlequin shrimp, Coleman/tiger shrimp, boxer crabs, frogfish, ghost pipefish, pygmy seahorses, blue-spotted stingrays, mimic octopus, moray eels. Night: Spanish dancers. NOTE: No airplane frame exists at this site - earlier description was in error.

tags.name: Liberty Muck
tags.description: Black sand muck area adjacent to USAT Liberty wreck at Tulamben. Shore entry. Sand ridges perpendicular to shore, 12-28m. Only 2 sources found - requires additional independent validation for 3-source requirement.

tags.name: Kubu Reef
tags.description: Kubu village, 3.5km NW of Tulamben. Sandy ridges with coral outcrops shallowing to deeper reef with small wall beyond 30m. Two sub-sites. Anglerfish (multiple color morphs), pygmy seahorses on gorgonias, ghost pipefish, barrel sponges. Local porters assist with equipment. Shore entry over rocky shoreline.

tags.name: Kubu Wreck
tags.description: Boga Wreck - Dutch-built Indonesian government patrol vessel ~40-45m length, intentionally sunk September 2012 as artificial reef off Kubu village. Bow ~18m, stern 38-40m (has settled deeper over time). Deck props: non-original wooden steering wheel, Buddha statues, VW car, scuba tanks. Confirmed species: moray eels, barracuda, batfish, lionfish, nudibranchs, octopus, turtles. Gorgonian fans colonising structure.

tags.name: Jemeluk Wall
tags.description: East end of Jemeluk Beach, Amed. Slope steepens to wall dropping to 50m+. Upper slope: small gorgonias, barrel sponges, large pink gorgonia fans, hard corals. Moray eels in crevices, ribbon eels in sandy areas, clownfish in anemones on upper slope. Wall top at ~15m. Shore entry from beach. ~10 min from Hotel Uyah. Intermediate to Advanced due to depth.

tags.name: Lipah Bay
tags.description: ~12 min from Amed. Sandy slope to 40m with artificial reef structures (coral-colonised), natural coral patches, large bommies with gorgonias and barrel sponges. Confirmed shark nursery area - baby and juvenile reef sharks shelter under large table corals alongside adult reef sharks. Hawksbill and green turtles regular. Large moray eels. Shore entry over black pebble beach. All levels when calm. Site_type corrected from wall to reef.

tags.name: Barracuda Wall
tags.description: Drift dive from east end of Bunutan Beach heading east-southeast. Wall densely covered in thousands of barrel sponges and hundreds of gorgonia fans. Large garden eel colonies on sandy bottom - among largest in Amed area. Ribbon eels and moray eels in wall crevices. Very large brain corals. Current-assisted drift; boat follows bubbles for pickup. Note: only 2 sources found.
